claim_text: China-origin electric vehicles (fully electric and plug-in hybrid passenger vehicles) face a 100 percent Section 301 tariff, increased from 25 percent, effective August 1, 2024. This rate remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Final rule published in Federal Register; Section 301 USTR four-year review finalized, rate confirmed in multiple official sources including USTR and CBP guidance.

claim_text: China-origin semiconductors including diodes, transistors, electronic integrated circuits and parts face a 50 percent Section 301 tariff, increased from 25 percent, effective January 1, 2025. This rate remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Final rule published by USTR; Section 301 four-year review finalized with specific effective dates confirmed in Federal Register notices.

claim_text: China-origin solar cells face a 50 percent Section 301 tariff, increased from 25 percent, effective in 2024. This rate remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Final rule published by USTR as part of Section 301 four-year strategic review, confirmed effective September 27, 2024.

claim_text: China-origin lithium-ion EV batteries face a 25 percent Section 301 tariff, increased from 7.5 percent, effective September 27, 2024. This rate remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Final rule published by USTR as part of Section 301 four-year review with confirmed effective date of September 27, 2024.

claim_text: China-origin steel and aluminum products face a 25 percent Section 301 tariff effective September 27, 2024, in addition to the existing 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the metal content, resulting in stacked duties on Chinese steel and aluminum as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 four-year review final rule confirmed 25 percent rate on steel and aluminum products from China effective September 27, 2024.

claim_text: China-origin consumer goods, furniture, lighting, bags, and textiles (Section 301 List 3) face a 7.5 percent tariff. This rate has been in place since February 2020 and remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 List 3 reduced from 25 percent to 7.5 percent in February 2020 as part of Phase 1 trade deal; rate confirmed unchanged through multiple USTR reviews.

claim_text: China-origin apparel, footwear, and consumer electronics (Section 301 List 4A) face a 7.5 percent tariff. This rate has been in place since February 2020 and remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 List 4A reduced from 15 percent to 7.5 percent in February 2020 during Phase 1 US-China trade deal negotiations; rate confirmed unchanged through March 2026.

claim_text: China-origin industrial machinery, electronics components, and robotics (Section 301 List 1) face a 25 percent tariff. This rate has been in force since July 2018 and remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 List 1 tariff has been in continuous force since July 2018; never rescinded, confirmed operative as of March 2026 by USTR and CBP.

claim_text: China-origin medical gloves face a 100 percent Section 301 tariff effective January 1, 2026, increased from 50 percent in 2025 and 25 percent prior to that. Rate confirmed in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 four-year review published scheduled rate increases for medical gloves: 50 percent in 2025, 100 percent in 2026, confirmed in Federal Register final rule.

claim_text: China-origin syringes and needles face a 100 percent Section 301 tariff effective September 27, 2024. Rate confirmed in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 four-year review final rule confirmed 100 percent tariff on syringes and needles from China effective September 27, 2024.

claim_text: China-origin lithium-ion non-EV batteries face a 25 percent Section 301 tariff effective January 1, 2026. Rate confirmed in force as of March 2026.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 301 four-year review final rule published scheduled 25 percent tariff on non-EV lithium-ion batteries from China effective January 1, 2026.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from China face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of all covered products, effective June 4, 2025. This rate replaced the prior 25 percent rate and applies to China along with most other trading partners (except the UK at 25%).
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation issued June 3, 2025 under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962; effective June 4, 2025; not invalidated by Supreme Court IEEPA ruling.

claim_text: Canada's non-USMCA-compliant goods face a 25 percent tariff. As of March 5-6, 2026, USMCA-compliant imports (approximately 88 percent of Canadian imports) received exemptions from this tariff, while non-USMCA goods remain subject to 25 percent.
subject: Canada
certainty_rationale: Executive order imposed 25 percent tariff on Canada February 1, 2025; took effect March 4, 2025; USMCA exemptions confirmed in subsequent presidential proclamations.

claim_text: Canada's energy and energy products not qualifying under USMCA face a 10 percent tariff. This lower rate for energy applies as of March 2026 following presidential proclamations adjusting the original tariff structure.
subject: Canada
certainty_rationale: Presidential proclamation lowered non-USMCA Canadian energy tariff to 10 percent, confirmed by Canadian government official publications.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from Canada face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of all covered products, effective June 4, 2025. The tariff applies to Canadian steel and aluminum not exempted under USMCA.
subject: Canada
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; Canada confirmed subject to 50 percent rate on steel and aluminum content.

claim_text: Canada faces a 10 percent Section 232 tariff on softwood lumber imports effective October 14, 2025, stacking with existing anti-dumping and countervailing duties that already apply to Canadian softwood lumber.
subject: Canada
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation under Section 232 with specific HTSUS codes, effective date confirmed in official trade compliance tracker.

claim_text: Mexico's non-USMCA-compliant goods face a 25 percent tariff effective March 4, 2025. USMCA-compliant imports remain exempt from this tariff with exemptions extended indefinitely as of April 2025. Auto imports were separately exempted until April 2, 2026.
subject: Mexico
certainty_rationale: Executive order imposed 25 percent tariff on Mexico February 1, 2025; took effect March 4, 2025; USMCA exemptions confirmed in subsequent presidential proclamations.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from Mexico face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of all covered products, effective June 4, 2025. The rate applies to Mexican steel and aluminum not exempted under USMCA.
subject: Mexico
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; Mexico confirmed subject to 50 percent rate on steel and aluminum content.

claim_text: Automobiles and automobile parts from the European Union face a 15 percent combined tariff rate (MFN plus Section 232) effective retroactively from August 1, 2025, reduced from the prior 27.5 percent rate under the US-EU Framework on Reciprocal, Fair, and Balanced Trade.
subject: European Union
certainty_rationale: Federal Register rule published September 25, 2025 implementing the US-EU Framework Agreement tariff provisions; 15 percent combined auto rate confirmed with retroactive August 1, 2025 effective date.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from the European Union face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products, effective June 4, 2025. Steel and aluminum are explicitly excluded from the US-EU Framework Agreement's 15 percent auto rate.
subject: European Union
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent; EU explicitly noted as subject to 50 percent rate; US-EU Framework Agreement confirmed steel and aluminum excluded from its automotive tariff reductions.

claim_text: Germany (as part of the European Union) faces a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on steel and aluminum products and a 15 percent combined rate on automobiles and automobile parts effective August 1, 2025 under the US-EU Framework Agreement.
subject: Germany
certainty_rationale: Germany subject to EU-wide tariff framework; US-EU Framework Agreement Federal Register rule confirmed 15 percent auto rate and 50 percent steel/aluminum rate for all EU member states.

claim_text: Japan faces a 15 percent baseline tariff on most imports under the US-Japan Framework Agreement effective August 7, 2025. This rate is inclusive of MFN tariffs and replaces the threatened 25 percent reciprocal rate. Japanese automotive imports face a 15 percent combined tariff, reduced from 27.5 percent.
subject: Japan
certainty_rationale: Federal Register rule published September 16, 2025 implementing the US-Japan Agreement tariff provisions; 15 percent baseline rate confirmed effective August 7, 2025.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from Japan face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products, effective June 4, 2025. Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum are not reduced by the US-Japan Framework Agreement.
subject: Japan
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent; Japan subject to 50 percent rate; US-Japan Framework Agreement confirmed Section 232 steel and aluminum tariffs remain at 50 percent and are not modified by the agreement.

claim_text: South Korea faces a 15 percent tariff on automobiles and automobile parts effective November 1, 2025, and a 15 percent tariff on other covered goods effective November 14, 2025, under the US-Korea Strategic Trade and Investment Deal. Goods with MFN tariff below 15 percent face a combined rate capped at 15 percent.
subject: South Korea
certainty_rationale: Federal Register rule published December 4, 2025 implementing the US-Korea Strategic Trade and Investment Deal; 15 percent auto rate retroactive to November 1, 2025 and 15 percent for other goods effective November 14, 2025 confirmed.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from South Korea face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products, effective June 4, 2025.
subject: South Korea
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; South Korea subject to this rate.

claim_text: Vietnam faces a 20 percent tariff on general imports following a reduction from the original 46 percent reciprocal rate. A 40 percent tariff applies to goods determined to be transshipped through Vietnam from third countries. Under the replacement Section 122 framework effective February 24, 2026, Vietnam now faces a 10 percent baseline surcharge along with applicable MFN duties.
subject: Vietnam
certainty_rationale: Vietnam tariff rate reduction from 46 percent to 20 percent announced and reported by Tax Foundation tracker; transshipment rate of 40 percent confirmed in official announcements.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from Vietnam face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products, effective June 4, 2025.
subject: Vietnam
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; Vietnam subject to this rate.

claim_text: India's IEEPA reciprocal tariff was negotiated down from 50 percent to 18 percent as of February 6, 2026, following India's agreement to stop purchasing Russian oil and commit to buying USD 500 billion in US products. Following the SCOTUS IEEPA ruling on February 20, 2026, India now faces the 10 percent Section 122 baseline surcharge plus applicable MFN duties.
subject: India
certainty_rationale: India-US tariff negotiations confirmed by executive order February 6, 2026; subsequent SCOTUS ruling February 20, 2026 eliminated IEEPA authority; India now subject to Section 122 10 percent baseline.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from India face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products, effective June 4, 2025.
subject: India
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; India confirmed subject to this rate.

claim_text: Taiwan's IEEPA reciprocal tariff was negotiated down to 15 percent as of a January 15, 2026 trade deal announcement, reduced from the prior 20 percent rate. Following the SCOTUS IEEPA ruling on February 20, 2026, Taiwan now faces the 10 percent Section 122 baseline surcharge plus applicable MFN duties.
subject: Taiwan
certainty_rationale: Taiwan trade deal reducing rate from 20 percent to 15 percent confirmed by executive order January 2026; SCOTUS ruling February 20, 2026 ended IEEPA tariff authority; Taiwan now subject to Section 122 10 percent baseline.

claim_text: China-origin advanced logic semiconductor chips face an additional 25 percent Section 232 tariff effective January 15, 2026, stacking with existing Section 301 tariffs of 50 percent on semiconductors, resulting in very high combined rates on Chinese-origin advanced chips.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation under Section 232 targeting advanced logic semiconductors; described with specific HTSUS codes and exemptions in official White House documentation effective January 15, 2026.

claim_text: A 10 percent global baseline import surcharge under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 applies to nearly all imports from all countries effective February 24, 2026, for 150 days until July 24, 2026. This surcharge replaced IEEPA tariffs after the Supreme Court ruling and does not stack with Section 232 tariffs.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation signed February 20, 2026 invoking Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974; 10 percent surcharge confirmed by CBP guidance and multiple law firm analyses.

claim_text: A 10 percent global baseline import surcharge under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 applies to imports from Vietnam effective February 24, 2026, for 150 days, replacing the higher IEEPA reciprocal tariff. This is the baseline rate; Section 232 tariffs on steel/aluminum are separate and excluded from the surcharge.
subject: Vietnam
certainty_rationale: Section 122 Presidential Proclamation applies universally to all countries; confirmed by White & Case and multiple legal sources that Vietnam is now subject to 10 percent surcharge replacing higher IEEPA rates.

claim_text: A 10 percent global baseline import surcharge under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 applies to imports from India effective February 24, 2026, for 150 days, replacing the 18 percent negotiated IEEPA reciprocal tariff. This is the baseline rate; Section 232 tariffs on steel/aluminum are separate.
subject: India
certainty_rationale: Section 122 Presidential Proclamation applies universally; India confirmed subject to 10 percent baseline surcharge after SCOTUS ruling ended IEEPA authority including negotiated 18 percent India rate.

claim_text: A 10 percent global baseline import surcharge under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 applies to imports from Bangladesh effective February 24, 2026, for 150 days. Bangladesh apparel and textiles face this 10 percent surcharge stacked on top of regular MFN duties.
subject: Bangladesh
certainty_rationale: Section 122 Presidential Proclamation applies universally to all countries including Bangladesh; confirmed by White & Case analysis; CAFTA-DR apparel exemption does not apply to Bangladesh.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from Turkey face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products, effective June 4, 2025. Turkey was previously subject to a higher 200 percent rate on steel before tariff restructuring in early 2025.
subject: Turkey
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; Turkey subject to 50 percent rate on steel and aluminum content.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from Brazil face a 50 percent Section 232 tariff on the steel and aluminum content of covered products effective June 4, 2025. Note: earlier reports of a lower 10 percent rate for Brazil appear to reflect pre-June 2025 tariff-rate quota arrangements that were terminated.
subject: Brazil
certainty_rationale: Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 increased Section 232 tariffs to 50 percent for all countries except UK; prior TRQ arrangements for Brazil terminated March 12, 2025 per Sandler Travis & Rosenberg analysis.

claim_text: Steel and aluminum imports from the United Kingdom face a 25 percent Section 232 tariff, which is half the rate applied to most other countries. The UK rate was set at 25 percent under the US-UK Economic Prosperity Deal announced May 8, 2025 and remains in force as of March 2026.
subject: United Kingdom
certainty_rationale: US-UK Economic Prosperity Deal announced May 8, 2025 set UK Section 232 steel and aluminum rate at 25 percent; confirmed by Presidential Proclamation June 3, 2025 which set 50 percent for all countries except UK.

claim_text: Russia-origin aluminum and aluminum derivative products face a 200 percent Section 232 tariff, a punitive rate that has been in place as a sanction and trade measure and is explicitly maintained even as other countries were moved to the standard 50 percent rate in June 2025.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: 200 percent tariff on Russian aluminum confirmed in Presidential Proclamation analysis; explicitly maintained when broader Section 232 tariffs were restructured in June 2025.

claim_text: Upholstered furniture imports face a 25 percent Section 232 tariff effective October 14, 2025, increasing to 30 percent on January 1, 2027. Lower rates apply for specific trade-deal partners: 10 percent for UK, 15 percent for EU, Japan, and South Korea. China faces the 25 percent rate given its Section 301 tariffs also apply.
subject: China
certainty_rationale: Section 232 Presidential Proclamation on upholstered furniture confirmed effective October 14, 2025; differentiated rates for trade-deal partners confirmed in official tracker.
